LEGAL BOUNDARY · DATA-ZERO POSTURE · PUBLIC-SAFE BY DEFAULT
GoalOS AGIJobManagerLegal Boundary
HomePrivacyTermsRegulatoryAGIALPHA BoundaryResponsibilityAssurance
Third-party responsibility

Wallets, chains, and providers
are independent surfaces.

Users choose and control their wallets, RPC providers, browsers, GitHub activity, blockchain actions, and third-party services.

GitHub

Repository and Pages host.

GitHub hosts the repository and the static website. GitHub's own policies govern GitHub account activity, issue/PR activity, and hosting-level technical processing.

Wallets and RPC

User-selected providers.

Wallet providers and RPC providers are independent. They may process addresses, requests, signatures, metadata, and transaction data according to their own terms and policies.

Ethereum Mainnet

Public and irreversible.

Ethereum transactions may be public, permanent, expensive, irreversible, and visible to third parties. The user is responsible for understanding this before signing.

Operator checklist

Review before action.

Verify addresses, chain ID, calldata, allowance, gas, terms, jurisdiction, tax, sanctions, securities, privacy, procurement, and internal approval before any wallet operation.
